where_to_eat.name: Casa do Alentejo
where_to_eat.description: A restaurant hidden behind a door on Rua das Portas de Santo Antão, one of Lisbon's busiest streets near the famous ginginha bars. It features a full dining establishment serving traditional Portuguese cuisine.

where_to_eat.name: Ginginha Bars
where_to_eat.description: Traditional bars located on Rua das Portas de Santo Antão serving ginginha, a famous Portuguese cherry liqueur. A local favorite for a quick traditional drink experience.

where_to_eat.name: Pastel de Nata
where_to_eat.description: Portugal's incredible pastries, particularly the famous Pastel de Nata (custard tarts), are a must-try. These sweet treats are beloved throughout Lisbon and well worth seeking out.

into_the_wild.name: Alfama Waterfront
into_the_wild.description: The charming waterfront area of Alfama is perfect for biking and exploring. The neighborhood offers beautiful paths along the water with stunning riverside views.

into_the_wild.name: Castelo de São Jorge
into_the_wild.description: A hilltop castle that rises above the Alfama rooftops with commanding views over Lisbon, the Tagus River, and the cascading pastel-colored buildings with red tile roofs.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Alfama Neighborhood
gram_worthy_spots.description: The old historic neighborhood with cascading pastel-colored buildings, red tile roofs, steep streets, and charming tiled buildings that photograph beautifully from multiple angles.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Castelo de São Jorge Viewpoint
gram_worthy_spots.description: One of Lisbon's most spectacular observation decks offering gorgeous views of the castle rising above Alfama rooftops, the Tagus River, and the warm light catching red tiles.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Lisbon Street Art Spots
gram_worthy_spots.description: Throughout the city you can find impressive street art by renowned artists like Ogringo and OzeArv. Portugal has excellent street art that makes for great photographic moments.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Príncipe Real Neighborhood
gram_worthy_spots.description: A lively neighborhood worth exploring on foot, featuring charming corners, tiled buildings, and atmospheric streets perfect for capturing the essence of Lisbon's character.

off_the_beaten_path.name: Lisbon's Oldest House (XVII Century)
off_the_beaten_path.description: Tucked away from the main tourist routes on a quiet street, this 17th-century house has survived the 1755 earthquake, multiple rebuilds, and centuries of change. Now part of the cityscape, it represents living history.

off_the_beaten_path.name: Underground Fado Sessions in Alfama
off_the_beaten_path.description: Local hosts in Alfama's old neighborhood occasionally run intimate, underground fado music sessions in their homes. These authentic Portuguese musical experiences offer genuine cultural immersion away from tourist venues.

off_the_beaten_path.name: Monsanto
off_the_beaten_path.description: A uniquely beautiful small sleepy town about 4 hours from Lisbon near the Spain border, featuring houses built into and under massive boulders. An off-the-beaten-path gem that delights at every corner.
